attributes.stem: A 45-year-old man with a family history of premature cardiovascular disease has elevated cholesterol levels. His doctor recommends dietary modifications for primary prevention.
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the following best describes the effect of dietary modifications on disease pathophysiology?
options: Garlic consumption provides clinically significant protection against atherosclerosis
Caloric restriction extends lifespan by activating telomerase enzymes
Omega-3 fatty acid supplements significantly reduce cardiovascular disease risk
Dietary fibre prevents colon cancer by binding to carcinogenic bile acids
Changing the ratio of saturated to polyunsaturated fats reduces serum cholesterol
attributes.explanation:
The correct answer is that changing the ratio of saturated to polyunsaturated fats reduces serum cholesterol. When the level of saturated fatty acids is lowered to match the level of polyunsaturated fatty acids (changing from the typical 3:1 ratio), serum cholesterol levels can be reduced by 10-15% within weeks. This modification represents an evidence-based dietary intervention for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease.

The incorrect options can be explained as follows:

  • Omega-3 fatty acid supplements significantly reduce cardiovascular disease risk - While omega-3 fatty acids were once thought to be protective, large meta-analyses of randomised controlled trials have shown that dietary supplements of omega-3 fatty acids or consumption of oily fish have little to no effect on cardiovascular disease outcomes such as ischaemic heart disease or stroke.
  • Caloric restriction extends lifespan by activating telomerase enzymes - While caloric restriction has been demonstrated to increase lifespan in experimental animals including primates, the specific mechanism is not clearly established. The statement about telomerase activation is not supported by evidence.
  • Garlic consumption provides clinically significant protection against atherosclerosis - Although garlic has been proposed to have cardiovascular benefits, research has not unequivocally proven this effect. The clinical significance of any potential benefit remains questionable.
  • Dietary fibre prevents colon cancer by binding to carcinogenic bile acids - While dietary fibre (roughage) is thought to have a preventive effect against diverticulosis of the colon by increasing faecal bulk, the specific mechanism described regarding colon cancer prevention through bile acid binding is not established as the primary mechanism.

Dietary modifications can significantly impact disease pathophysiology. Other established relationships include sodium restriction reducing hypertension and increased dietary fibre helping prevent diverticulosis. The relationship between diet and disease is an important area of preventive medicine, particularly for conditions like atherosclerosis where modifiable risk factors play a significant role.
learningPoints: Dietary composition significantly influences the development and progression of various diseases, even in the absence of malnutrition.
Reducing saturated fatty acid consumption and increasing polyunsaturated fatty acids can lower serum cholesterol levels by 10-15% within weeks.
Dietary modifications such as sodium restriction, increased fibre intake, and caloric restriction have specific effects on various disease processes.
references: Kumar V, Abbas AK, Aster JC. Robbins and Cotran Pathologic Basis of Disease. 10th ed. Philadelphia: Elsevier; 2020. Chapter 9; page 450.
